BREAKING - Italian medical chief dies after contracting #COVID19. The head of the medical association in the northern Italian province of Varese, Roberto Stella, 67, has died of respiratory failure after contracting coronavirus.

He had been practicing medicine at an outpatient clinic in Busto Arsizio, north-west of Milan, and Italian reports say he and a colleague were infected at the weekend.

The more than 600 deaths associated with the coronavirus that have taken place in Italy is added this Friday by Roberto Stella, one of the first two infected doctors in the country.

Stella, 67, worked in the general medicine department and was president of the Varese College of Physicians in Lombardy. He was admitted to the Como hospital for respiratory failure and finally died on Tuesday night after confirming the positive for coronavirus a few days earlier.

Italy has banned the exits and entrances of the country to try to avoid the spread of the virus, since it is one of the most affected areas with more than 10,140 cases and 630 deaths.

People in Italy can go out and move around the territory, but only if they can justify doing so for work, health or necessity.

The Government's decision to extend the restrictive measures throughout the country has already had the first immediate consequences: the Vatican has closed the square and the Basilica of San Pietro until April 3.

While British Airways and Ryanair airlines announced Tuesday that they cancel their flights to and from Italy, countries such as Spain, Portugal Malta, Austria and Serbia have announced actions to block their connections with the country.
